data.title: Ethereum Foundation and Keyring Network Launch Privacy-Focused Financing Mechanism
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the Ethereum Foundation's funding coordination team has partnered with Keyring Network to introduce a new financing mechanism aimed at supporting privacy developers. The zkVerified permissioned vault on Keyring's mainnet offers access to a compliant and privacy-protecting DeFi lending market. During the initial two months, all protocol fees from the Keyring zkVerified vault will be donated to the legal defense fund for Roman Storm and Alexey Pertsev, developers of Tornado Cash, who are currently on trial for releasing open-source code.

The Ethereum Foundation emphasized that this model ensures the first users of the vault can directly support the legal protection of developers focused on privacy. Keyring connects the growth of new financial tools with the protection of developers, demonstrating that the community can enhance resilience while driving innovation.

data.title: Cango: Mining output this week was 135.6 BTC, bringing the total Bitcoin holdings to 5985.4
data.description: Cango posted on the X platform that mining produced 135.6 BTC this week, bringing the total Bitcoin holdings to 5,985.4.

data.title: Kalshi raises $300 million at $5 billion valuation
data.description: According to market news: Kalshi raised $300 million at a valuation of $5 billion.

data.title: Solana Company Reaches Custody Agreement with Coinbase, Anchorage Digital, and Others
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Solana Company (formerly Helius Medical Technologies) announced that it has reached custody agreements with Coinbase, BitGo, and Anchorage Digital to support its digital asset treasury strategy and the continued accumulation of Solana's native asset, SOL. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Prestige Wealth, a US-listed company, has completed approximately US$150 million in financing to launch XAUT Treasury.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Prestige Wealth Inc. announced that it has participated in and completed a series of synergistic transactions to launch Nasdaq's first Tether Gold (XAUT) treasury. These transactions included approximately $100 million in private equity investment (PIPE) financing from Antalpha Platform Holding Company and other accredited investors, including Tether and Kiara Capital, as well as a three-year, $50 million senior debt financing. The company intends to use the majority of the net proceeds to purchase Tether Gold (XAUT) as a reserve asset. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Analyst: Bitcoin is fluctuating at a high level, and the market trend remains bullish
data.description: Timothy Misir, head of research at BRN, said: "The current Bitcoin market is in a high-volatility, calm, liquid, and quietly bullish phase. Institutional capital flows remain the mainstay of this phase, and ETFs serve as a liquidity bridge between traditional and digital finance. Although there will be short-term fluctuations, the macro liquidity wave, enterprise adoption and structural capital inflows all indicate that the market will continue to develop." (CoinDesk)

data.title: Russia to allow banks to conduct cryptocurrency business, but with strict restrictions
data.description: Vladimir Chistyukhin, First Deputy Governor of the Central Bank of Russia, said that the Central Bank of Russia will allow banks to conduct cryptocurrency business, but will impose strict capital restrictions and reserve requirements. At the same time, it plans to implement "quite strict requirements" to prevent cryptocurrency business from becoming the dominant activity of banking institutions, including limiting banks' cryptocurrency exposure to 1% of their capital. (Cryptopolitan)

data.title: Cango's Bitcoin Mining Output Reaches 135.6 BTC This Week
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S.-listed Bitcoin mining company Cango (CANG) reported a mining output of 135.6 BTC this week. The company's total Bitcoin holdings have now reached 5,985.4 BTC.

data.title: Abu Dhabi Airport to pilot stablecoin and cryptocurrency payments
data.description: Abu Dhabi Airports and Al Hail Holding, an Abu Dhabi investment entity, have signed a memorandum of understanding to explore next-generation payment solutions, including stablecoins and cryptocurrencies/digital assets. Al Hail Holding, which previously invested in digital bank Zand, and Abu Dhabi Airports will also collaborate to develop a regulated digital wallet for inbound passengers arriving at Zayed International Airport, enhancing the UAE's position as a global hub for tourism, fintech, and sustainable transportation. The collaboration will also cover the integration of artificial intelligence systems and the development of sustainable infrastructure. (Cryptopolitan)

data.title: Binance: Users holding at least 200 points can claim the Binance Alpha airdrop at 9 PM tonight
data.description: Binance announced the Binance Alpha airdrop, which will begin at 9:00 PM (UTC+8) today. Users holding at least 200 Binance Alpha Points are eligible to claim the tokens. The airdrop will be distributed on a first-come, first-served basis until the airdrop pool is fully redeemed or the event ends. Further details will be announced later.

data.title: The Chinese meme token "Open Sesame" has surpassed 1 million in market capitalization, with the top holding address seeing a profit exceeding 8189.72%.
data.description: According to data from the Gate Web 3 platform, the market capitalization of "Open Sesame," a Chinese meme token launched by Gate Fun, has surpassed 1 million. The token has seen a surge of over 4,800% since its launch, with the top holder purchasing 10,797,600 tokens at $170,7948 USD, representing a current floating profit exceeding 8,189.72%.

data.title: Fed Governor Waller: Open to a 25 basis point rate cut at upcoming meeting
data.description: Federal Reserve Board Governor Waller said he is open to a 25 basis point rate cut at the upcoming meeting. The Fed needs to cut interest rates, but it needs to do so with caution. (Jinshi)

data.title: Solana: The core of competition in crypto networks is the efficiency of matching supply and demand. Meme coins and NFTs have expanded the basic concept of assets.
data.description: Solana stated in a post on its official X account: "All assets will eventually become digital. Tokenization replaces paper-based ownership with cryptographic ownership, fundamentally ensuring asset security and globalization. Tokenization also makes all assets compatible with stablecoins and DeFi. This is why institutions and asset custodians are turning to cryptocurrencies." So-called "user-generated assets" such as meme coins, creator tokens, and NFTs are all native internet assets, expanding the basic concept of an asset. However, they can only be considered assets if they are tradable; if they are not, they cannot be valued. The easier it is to trade an asset, the easier it is for sellers to find buyers, and the greater the liquidity. Therefore, every crypto network will ultimately compete on the efficiency of matching supply and demand. Ultimately, it comes down to the technology, the products built on top of the crypto network, and the resulting community.

data.title: Bitget Q3 report: UEX strategy has integrated core sectors such as on-chain, US stocks and AI trading
data.description: Bitget released its third-quarter transparency report, revealing key progress in product integration, on-chain expansion, and user growth. The report reveals that with the implementation of its "Universal Exchange UEX" strategy, Bitget has achieved multi-functional integration across spot trading, futures, on-chain assets, US stocks, and AI-powered trading. The on-chain trading segment has undergone a comprehensive upgrade, with the platform pioneering full asset coverage across four major public chains: ETH, SOL, BSC, and Basecoin. This supports trading of millions of on-chain assets, with daily trading volume exceeding $113 million. New AI tools enable real-time tracking of high-quality address activity and provide token alerts. In the derivatives trading sector, according to CoinDesk data, Bitget's average monthly trading volume reached $750 billion, with cumulative derivatives trading exceeding $11.5 trillion. Bitget ranks first in spot liquidity for ETH and SOL, and second for BTC.

data.title: Fed Governor Waller: CPI data will be very helpful for the upcoming meeting
data.description: Fed Governor Waller said the CPI data will be helpful for the upcoming meeting, and the ADP data is consistent with the Labor Department data, both pointing to negative job growth.

data.title: Greeks.live: The overall sentiment in the community is pessimistic, and BTC has orders supporting it at $120,000.
data.description: Adam, a researcher at Greeks.live, wrote on the X platform, "Overall community sentiment is pessimistic, with traders believing the cryptocurrency market is underperforming US stocks, A-shares, and gold. Most traders are disappointed with ETH's weak performance. Regarding key support levels, BTC has a large number of orders at $120,000, while ETH has repeatedly breached $4,400 during trading. The community is divided on whether we are still in a bull market, with some traders expressing their intention to shift to US stocks after the next bear market."

data.title: Crypto fund C1 Fund announced that it has purchased Ripple shares
data.description: Crypto fund C1 Fund announced it has purchased equity in enterprise blockchain solutions provider Ripple, but has not yet disclosed the specific purchase amount and equity percentage. C1 Fund reportedly previously announced a $60 million IPO and plans to increase its investment in digital asset technology. (Businesswire)

data.title: YZi Labs: The second season of the EASY Residency global incubation program will begin next week
data.description: YZi Labs announced on the X platform that the second season of its EASY Residency global incubation program will begin next week.

data.title: Research shows that the threat of BTC "51% attack" is underestimated, and attackers only need about $6 billion to destroy the network
data.description: Recent research suggests that the threat of a "51% attack" facing Bitcoin is significantly underestimated by the market, and that an attacker would only need approximately $6 billion to destroy Bitcoin. Duke University finance professor Campbell Harvey warned in a new study that while both Bitcoin and gold are considered favorites in the "currency devaluation trade," Bitcoin faces far greater risks. An attacker could seize control of the Bitcoin network within a week by purchasing $4.6 billion worth of hardware, investing $1.34 billion in data center construction, and adding approximately $130 million in weekly electricity costs. By shorting Bitcoin in the derivatives market, an attacker could reap significant profits when the price plummets, potentially covering the cost of the attack. However, Matt Prusak, president of Bitcoin USA, believes these concerns are overblown, citing the years required to accumulate and deploy mining equipment, the substantial collateral required for short selling, and the potential for exchanges to suspend suspicious transactions. (Wall Street Journal)

data.title: Plasma’s XPL token fell below its issue price, now trading at 0.7175 USDT
data.description: Market News: Plasma's XPL token fell below its issue price, currently trading at 0.7175 USDT, and the enthusiasm for Tether's support has subsided.
